title: Hubert Strang vom Palästinakorps auf seinem Weg nach Jerusalem und zurück nach Wilhelmshaven | Hubert Strang of Palestinecorps on his way to Jerusalem and back to Wilhelmshaven
text: Extensive collection of postcards from the Palestine campaign, containing black-and-white and colored prints from Constantinople, Jerusalem and Damascus, among other places, documenting the corps' route; associated sales catalogue, the photographic art publisher Schiller & Janssen, Gütersloh i. Westf., contains the complete index of all 625 trading cards about the Palestine campaign.

title: FRAD033-090 Histoire d'Alphonse et Paul Gueytat | Documents from Alphonse et Paul Gueytat
text: Postcards and correspondence. Plan of the military cemetery. Regimental orders and sketches, notes, notebook, press article. Photographs. Double death notice.

title: FRAD017 - Histoire de la famille Lebrun | Famille Lebrun
text: A military booklet, a notebook of songs written during the war by another soldier, 1 quote after injuries (gas), a free care booklet, photographs

title: FRAD087_052-Histoire d'Antoine Malaty | Story of Antoine Malaty
text: - photograph of Antoine Malaty dressed as a mounted policeman (1910) - correspondence exchanged by Antoine Malaty with his family (1915, 1918-1919) - inter-allied victory medal (with diploma) and commemorative medal of the 1914 war- 1918

title: Hermann Staratzkys Kriegstagebuch "Der Große Krieg" | War diary of Hermann Staratzky "The Great War"
text: PDF document with the transcript of Hermann Staratzky's diary from July 26 to August 24, 1914.

title: ACA4640172_43_BOUDET_01 - Félix Boudet, officier et artiste | Félix Boudet, Officer and Artist
text: Picture of Boudet and Colleagues and three other hand drawn pictures

title: FRBNBU-103 Georges Kohn photographe des " chevaliers du ciel " | Documents from Beilin family
text: 184 aerial and terrestrial photographs, large and small format.

title: FRAD017_110_Georges Pissard | Georges Pissard
text: Madame Marie-Madeline Crespeau, the great-niece of Georges Pissard, came to bring photos of the Front taken by her great-uncle, particularly in the towns of Esnes (Nord) and Auvaucourt (Verdun).

title: FRBNBU-158 Constant Brun prisonnier en Allemagne | Constant Brun in Germany
text: - a double-sided photograph during his captivity in Germany (scanned in two separate documents, the front = 01, the back = 02)

title: FRAD087_007-Correspondance et carnet d'Alphonse Faure | Story of Alphonse Faure
text: Album of postcards exchanged between Alphonse Faure, his family and his friends; Alphonse Faure's war diary; fighter card

title: Henri-Louis Cadot, Mémoire transmise | Henri-Louis Cadot, memory transmitted
text: Full digitized version of the Road Book left by my paternal grandfather Henri-Louis Cadot of the 143rd RI. Photos of my grandfather and military documents.

title: Sanitätsmaterial, Auszeichnungen | Der Sanitätsfeldwebel Johannes Kühn an der Westfront
text: Syringe set of a medical sergeant and medal bar

title: FRAD067-123/2 Second album de Nicolas Hiessler (1884-1952), infirmier en Flandre et en Artois (armée allemande) | Album from Nicolas Hiessler, with photographs from the Western Front
text: 2 - Photographic album, approx. 28 x 21cm, 1916-1918

title: FRAD071-002 Claude BERNARD, du front à l'hôpital | Documents from Claude Bernard
text: Photographs, medals, diploma of honor of the war of 1914-1918.

title: FRAD067-122 Auguste Gresset annonce à ses proches la mort à son poste de combat du Franc-Comtois Alexis Antoine Billot, le 4 juillet 1915 | About the death of Alexis Antoine Billot
text: 3 letters from Auguste Gresset to Pierre Dubost, relation of Mme Billot to Lons-le-Saunier (Jura), 1915; 2 photographs of the tomb of Alexis Antoine Billot in Montauville (Meurthe-et-Moselle), sd, 1915; 1 postcard from the Montauville military cemetery (Meurthe-et-Moselle), sd, 1915; 2 military citations, 1919 and 1920

title: FRAD033-080 Histoire de René, Laurent Hueber | Documents from René, Laurent Hueber
text: René, Laurent Hueber was born on May 11, 1896 in Saint-Maur-des-Fossés. Mobilized against Germany on April 13, 1915, he belonged to the 4th section of the 9th company of the 59th infantry regiment. He died on April 18, 1917 in Sept-Saulx (Marne).

title: FRAD033-084 Histoire de Joseph Piquemal | Documents from Joseph Piquemal
text: Military booklet. Correspondence: army correspondence card addressed to Joseph Piquemal (November 11, 1914); letter from Commandant Chevassu's wife addressed to Joseph Piquemal (October 9, 1914). Miscellaneous documents: card of the Ariège Association of disabled, reformed, widows, orphans, ascendants and veterans of the Great War (1923); diploma of the so-called “victory” inter-allied medal; medical certificate explaining and attesting to the injury received during the war and its consequences (November 5, 1926).

title: FRAD077-141B, Charles-Philippe Françon du 133e Régiment d'Infanterie | Charles-Philippe Françon, of 133e Infantery Regiment.
text: 1- Military booklet, 2- Savings book.

title: FRAD087_005-Médailles décernées à Jean Collet | Documents from Jean Collet
text: Commemorative diploma of the First World War to which are attached the 1914-1918 war medal and the inter-allied victory medal awarded to Jean Collet.

title: FRAN-PA-206 Deux morts dans le Bois de la Gruerie | Photographs from Leleu and Marousez
text: Black and white photographs of Paul Leleu, Clara Leleu and her two daughters and Auguste Marousez, sd

title: FRAD087_056-Histoire d'Ernest Rolle-Milaguet | Story of Ernest Rolle-Milaguet
text: Ernest Rolle-Milaguet's travel diary (February 1916-September 1919); copy of a photograph by Ernest Rolle-Milaguet; citation at the orders of the regiment (1918)

title: FRAD066_065A Jules SIVIEUDE | Documents of Jules Sivieude
text: Jules Casimir Sivieude was the paternal grandfather of Jean-Paul Sivieude, husband of Simone Sivieude. Born in 1888, he married on February 19, 1912 and was then employed at the Comptoir d'Escompte in Perpignan. He was called up and served in the war as an ammunition sergeant.

title: FRAM - Albert Chatelais | Document from Albert Chatelais
text: 1 Christmas postcard 1 postcard with the photograph of Albert Chatelais 2 medical certificates of visit 1 transport order 1 certificate authorizing the wearing of the war wounded ribbon 2 citations to the order of the 124th Infantry Regiment

title: FRBNBU-102 Camille Victor Belmon assiste à une messe en pleine forêt d'Argonne | A mess in Argonne's forest in 1917
text: - Photo of a mass in the Argonne forest in 1917 - photos with his regiment - cardboard with pasted photos - cardboard with pasted photo

title: FRBNBU-110 François Vendel "ma guerre", une blessure inguérissable | Francois Vendel's war, an incurable injury and wound
text: - Photo of François Vendel - War diary of François Vendel - Health report of François Vendel - Photo of the 13th Ci of the 172nd RI - Score of the patriotic song "La Française"

title: FRAD033-054 Histoire d’Edouard Dubart | Documents from Edouard Dubart
text: Account of Edouard Dubart's escape with its transcription; a map of the escape route; correspondence with his wife (1916-1917); a bust portrait of Edouart Dubart during his military service in 1898; a photograph of Edouart Dubart, his wife and two daughters in Valence; two photographs of groups in the camp; an escaped medal certificate (August 18, 1927).

title: FRAD066_067A Etienne PARES | Documents of Etienne PARES
text: Etienne PARES is the maternal great-grandfather of Marie-Françoise SCHMIDT. He was born in 1878 in Saint-Laurent-de-la-Salanque where he was a wheelwright. Alpine hunter, he was confined to the citadel of Perpignan in 1914, then to Grenoble. He fought on the Lorraine front in the 202nd Artillery Regiment in 1918.

title: Album mit Motiven von der Ost- und Westfront | Album with motives from Eastern- and Western Front
text: Photo album with motifs from the western front (from France) and from the eastern front (Russia).

title: Aquarelle vom Dorjan-See in Mazedonien | Aquarelles from Macedonia
text: The watercolors were painted by Joh. Siebert and show: (1) the Paljorka Monastery in South Macedonia (1916), (2) Lake Dojran (1916) and (3) a weapons workshop in the bivouac in South Macedonia (11.7.1916) .

title: Frans Van Sante in 'Den Grooten Oorlog' | Frans Van Sante in 'Den Grooten Oorlog'
text: I am in possession of the photo, which shows the family and the mare with the foal (probably taken around 1916-17?)

title: FRAD067-119 Cartes reçues de Wilhelm Keller (1873-1920) (armée allemande) par son épouse Salomé Eberhardt (1874-1955), à Bischheim (Bas-Rhin) | Postcards from Wilhelm Keller
text: 24 postcards (all parts preserved)

title: FRBMLY-60 Histoire de Fortuné Avid, sauvé par son livret militaire | Documents from Jean-Claude Avid
text: Military booklet, photo, cubit card

title: FRBNBU-095 Histoire du canonnier Henri Portret et de ses frères | Henri Portret was a guner
text: Octave Henri Portret, often called Henri, was born on January 21, 1889. He was 25 years old during the general mobilization and was in Argentina. Paris discount'. He was assigned as a gunner to the 5th heavy artillery regiment (RA) in Valence. He then passed to the 106th RA which received a citation in 1916. Henriette Portret, wife Madinan, her daughter, received from her father Henri some objects of the craftsmanship of the trenches brought back by Octave, as well as a collection of quite significant postcards of the time.

title: FRBMLY-63 Jean Noyer mon grand-père | Documents from PJ Rossignol
text: A photograph. A military booklet. the handwritten booklet. A frame.

title: FRBMBO-053 Histoire du soldat Alphonse Robine | Alphonse Robine's story
text: Main document. Selected extracts from a 164-page watercolor album covering the period 1915-1917.

title: Auguste, Joseph LECHANTEUX 1884-1950 | Documents of Auguste Joseph Lechanteux
text: LECHANTEUX, Auguste, Joseph, born in Chantenay, Sarthe, on January 23, 1884 - Class 1904, incorporated on October 10, 1905, incorporation number 383, Le Mans recruitment office, registration number 3873 - Private 1st class - 23 years old services - 5 campaigns - Wounded and cited: * to the order of the 317th Infantry Regiment, n° 292, November 4, 1916 * to the order of the Fourth Army – 4th Battalion, 317th Infantry Regiment, August 1 1917 * to Regimental Order No. 878 April 17, 1918 - Died 1950

title: FRBMLY-66 Histoire de mes grands parents Henri et Marie-Louise Mermin | Story of my grandparents
text: Correspondence between Henri, his wife and his family, photograph of the wedding of Henri and Marie-Louise and official telegram announcing his death.

title: Wilhelm Sandvoß´ Einsatz an der Ost- und Westfront | Wilhelm Sandvoß mission at Eastern and Western Front
text: War photos of Wilhelm Sandvoß from his deployment on the Eastern Front and a letter to his family dated February 25, 1916.

title: FRBMLY-71 Histoire de Georges Robart | Story of Georges Robart
text: Extract from his military booklet, his military aviator certificate, 2 notes relating to his last mission, 2 portraits of Georges Robart as well as a photo of him in front of his plane.

title: FRAM - Louis Cavil (1879-1922) | Documents from Louis Cavil
text: 1 photograph of Louis Cavil A letter addressed to Louis Cavil explaining to him the interview with the director of the Forges de Lochrist and the steps to be taken to obtain a job as a blacksmith, July 30, 1917.

title: FRBNBU-136 Emile Woirgard, lorrain, combat pour l'Allemagne | Emile Woirgard was german at the beginning of the war and french after the war
text: - Photo of Emile Woirgard in front of an artillery piece. - Verso of a photo: correspondence from the family of Emile Woirgard indicating his regiment - Gravestone map sent to his parents - Map showing the characteristics of a camp near a railway line.

title: FRBNBU-106 Histoire de Joseph Auguste Chenavier, grand-père de Guy Chevenet | Joseph Auguste Chenavier's story
text: 01 Joseph Auguste's military booklet. Copy of the page of his death. 02 Auguste Joseph Chenavier, 2 photos from 1914-1915. 03 Aeronautical photo album, war years 1914-18.

title: FRBMBO-070 Histoire du soldat Albéric Bru | Albéric Bru's story
text: Document: Three small notebooks (97 pages in all) which constitute a war diary between September 1914 and September 1915 from which it was necessary to select extracts.

title: Tagebuch des jungen Bernhard Bültemann aus Halle | The diary of young Bernhard Bültemann
text: War diary by Bernhard Bültemann. In it he describes both his father's military service, who died in 1915 in the East, as well as the general development of the war, school days during the war, the efforts of the women at home and at home, the harsh war winter of 1916/17 and the prisoner of war camp in Halle.

title: FRAD066_090 Barthélémy Garreta | Documents of Barthélémy Garreta
text: Photographs of Barthélémy Garreta with comrades (1st: standing left, 2nd: standing right, 3rd: seated); photograph of Barthélémy Garreta while he is a prisoner in Stuttgart and stamp on the back; commemorative card of the 92nd regiment.

title: FRAD043_060 Alexis Rispal, soldat de la Grande guerre | Documents about Alexis Rispal
text: 1/ Two photographic postcards by Antoine-Alexis Rispal, including one from February 1917. 2/ A photograph of Antoine-Alexis Rispal with three regimental comrades (Antoine-Alexis Rispal is the third from the left). 3/ Two photographs by Antoine-Alexis Rispal, one portrait and one bust.

title: FRAD058_021 M;Louis TOLLET, M. Etienne NOLOT | Documents from Mr Guy François Nolot
text: Mr. Guy François NOLOT lent documents concerning soldiers Louis TOLLET and Etienne NOLOT.

title: FRAD066_091 Georges Dubos et Marcel Vincent | Documents of Georges Dubos and Marcel Vincent
text: Travel diary by Georges Dubos (excerpts); photographs by Georges Dubos; photography by Marcel Vincent; letters from Marcel Vincent to his sister Yvonne (including one written at the signing of the Treaty of Versailles).

title: FRBNBU-119 Eugène Lucien Lebert cavalier au 15ème régiment de Chasseurs | Eugène Lucien Lebert was in the cavalery
text: 01 Eugene Lucien LEBERT. Military Medal Diploma. 02 Photo of the Widow of Chasseurs LEBERT and her 18-month-old daughter. 03 Trench art, vase engraved with holly leaves. 04 Engraved shell casing. 05 Military booklet (identity and occupation in civilian life).

title: Lettre du Commandant Ménétrier | Letter about Commandant Ménétrier
text: Documents of family origin: Letter written in May 1915 by Louis Ménifère to his father in Le Havre Letter written by a Mr. Martin about the death of Commander Ménifère Military document of summons to the order of the regiment by Lieutenant-Colonel Delagout , and citations to the order of the Army by Generals Sarrail and Langle de Cary

title: FRAD033-102 Histoire de Louis Gauthier | Documents from Louis Gauthier
text: Handwritten notebook "History of operations during the 1914-1918 war" accompanied by songs (1915). Small handwritten notebook of his life on the front and songs (1915). Notebook of songs written or copied during his convalescence in hospital and after the war (1918).

title: FRAM - Claude Alfred Camille VENOT | Claude VENOT
text: - Photograph of Claude VENOT - Service record of Lieutenant-Colonel Claude VENOT - Franchise card from Claude VENOT addressed to his wife (September 6, 1914) - Letter from du Mesnil to Claude VENOT's wife following his death (September 15, 1914) - Notes held by Claude VENOT in his hand when he was killed (September 7, 1914)

title: FRBNBU-108 Un suédois au service de la France | Knut William Mattson a physiotherapist in France service
text: Military Casualty Relief Certificate Certificate of Loan from Knut Mattson's Son Letter of Thanks from Halphen Rothschild Photo of Soldiers with Knut William Mattson

title: FRBNBU-135 Alphonse Colignon parti à la guerre à 41 ans | Alphonse Colignon's story
text: - 01 Alphonse Colignon. Military booklet. - 02 Alphonse Colignon. 2 Soldier's diplomas. Death on April 20, 1917. - 03 Alphonse Colignon. Enamelled plaque. Died in Pontavert (Aisne) at 44 years old. - 04 Alphonse Colignon and his brother Eugène. 2 pictures. - 05 Alphonse Colignon. Card to his family. - 06 Alphonse Colignon. 2 objects, one of which is engraved (each of the objects is made from 2 balls with their bottoms welded together).

title: FRAN-PA-112 Les frères Gravier | Documents from the brothers Gravier
text: Correspondence: letters from Jean to Gustave and letters from Gustave to his parents.

title: Mémoires d'un prisonnier : Abel Lugez : 1914-1919 | Memories of a prisoner of war
text: - Mobilization booklet - Handwritten, French / German lexicon in a school notebook - Correspondence of Abel with his family: photographs-postcards, letters - Quite a number of photos of comrades in captivity - Postcards of places: Chemnitz camp

title: FRAD087_064-Histoire de François Jandaud | Story of François Jandaud
text: François Jandaud's military booklet; postcard sent by François Jandaud to his wife on October 10, 1914.

title: FRAD087_062-"Notes de voyage" de Martial Daigueperse | Diary and documents from Martial Daigueperse
text: Two notebooks entitled "Notes de voyage" (August 7, 1914-March 26, 1919); military booklet; photograph by Martial Daigueperse taken before the war; his parents' family record book.

title: FRAD087_038-Histoire de Georges Philippon | Documents about Georges Philippon
text: Letter addressed to the mother of Georges Philippon dated September 10, 1915; letter from Constant to his brother Fernand from the hospital, undated.

title: FRAD077-139, Ernest Hylaire, correspondance avec sa plus jeune fille pendant la guerre. | Ernest Hylaire, letters to his younger daughter during the war.
text: 1- A photograph of Ernest Hylaire in soldier's uniform with his family, 2- A postcard with a humorous drawing, 3- A postcard from Marguerite to her father dated April 26, 1915 with an answer from Ernest Hylaire dated October 12, 1915 , 3- a postcard from Marguerite to her father dated September 14, 1915 with an answer from Ernest Hylaire, 4- a postcard from Marguerite and Suzanne to their father with an answer from Ernest Hylaire dated October 25, 1915, 5- a Postcard from Marguerite to her father dated December 29, 1916 with an answer from Ernest Hylaire.

title: FRBNBU-148 Photos souvenirs de guerre | Jean Pastour, cooker
text: A photo album with photographs of Jean Pastour during the war, a photo album with the environment of the war in 1914 and 1915 including a photo of a mass at the front and a military review.

title: Brief van Omer De Backer aan zijn vader, geschreven aan het front in Pervijze (12/11/1914) | Letter from Omer De Backer to his father, written from the front in Pervijze (12/11/1914)
text: Copy of a letter from Omer de Backer to his father Leopold (constable in Beveren aan de Yser) from the front in Pervijze on 12 November 1914 on the occasion of his name day (15 November)

title: FRAD043_064 La guerre de Jean Jouvenel, maréchal-ferrant | Documents from Jean Jouvenel
text: Portrait of Jean Jouvenel. Military quotes. History of the 36th and 236th field artillery regiments. Commemorative frame "1914-1918" accompanied by decorations and a portrait of Jean Jouvenel.

title: FRAM - Henri MESSEILLER | Henri MESSEILLER
text: - Photograph of the MESSEILLER family in front of their pharmacy, located in Asnières (Hauts-de-Seine). - Photograph of Henri MESSEILLER with his daughters. - Postcard from the staff of the hospital to which Henri MESSEILLER belonged.

title: FRAM - Albert et Charles Rauch | Documents from Albert and Charles Rauch
text: Postcard, censored letter with content of the censored part, song book, Licensee's Guide, quotes from Albert Rauch, photographs, excerpt from Marius CHIBRAN's military instruction book

title: FRBNBU-031 Vincent Burkarth et sa guerre | Vincent Burkarth's war
text: - Military booklet - Military quote from October 1915 - Two photographs/postcards of groups of soldiers

title: FRAD071-003, Jacques SAUVAGEOT, parcours d'un soldat. | Documents from Jacques Sauvageot
text: Set of photos and postcards, military booklet and a framed quote.

title: Des Poilus du Valdonnez | Documents belonging to Antoine Jean Francois Mejean
text: The documents are letters, postcards, portraits, statements, notebook...

title: FRAD033-126 Histoire de Marcel Geneste | Documents from Marcel Geneste
text: Correspondence with the main physiotherapy center of the 12th region (Limoges), with the chemical engineer P. Roubertie, with doctor Lomon from hospital 35 in Paris-plage (Pas-de-Calais). Suspension of mobilization. Technical description of the table. Photographs of the table.

title: FRAD033-079 Histoire de Pierre, Louis Mercé | Documents from Pierre Louis Mercé
text: Military booklet. Pass for Anna Mercé. Postcard (photograph) addressed to his parents (May 26, 1916). Program of the artistic evening (June 1, 1919). Song "The good little woman".

title: FRAD033-074 Histoire de Georges Coignet | Documents from Georges Coignet
text: Military booklet accompanied by its identification plate. 9 black and white photographs and postcards (1914-1918).

title: FRAD033-070 Histoire de René, Jean Dutruch | Documents from René Jean Dutruch
text: René, Jean Dutruch was born in 1896 in Toignan, commune of Saint-Loubès (Gironde). A soldier in the 25th company of the 57th infantry regiment, he joined the group of student corporals at Camp de Souge. During the conflict, he was injured and taken to the temporary hospital 46 Le Touquet - Paris-Plage (Pas-de-Calais).

title: AMSM-013_1 : Une enfance marquante | Letters and Picture Belonging to Joseph Pierre Antoine Eynard
text: A photograph of my soldier grandfather (1910) 4 letters 4 cards

title: Mes Souvenirs pendant mon Emigrage | Memorial diary of the relocation from Troissy (Bouquigny) Marne to Maizières la Grande Paroisse dans l'Aube
text: 1 PowerPoint document: My memories during my Emigration 1918.pps 1 PowerPoint document: My memories during my Emigration_recopie.pps

title: Pascal PISTON - Départ pour l'Italie en 1917, en passant par la Meuse | Pascal Piston's Letters, Pictures, and Maps from Italy
text: Maps of the front published and given to soldiers to write to their families Italian military documents - pass - permission Military booklet of Pascal PISTON born in 1884 Mobilization leaflet Postcards on both sides

title: FRAD067-206 Quelques bribes du parcours de Victor Heymes (La Wantzenau, Bas-Rhin, 1889-1979) (armée allemande) | Postcards from Victor Heymes
text: 4 postcards or photo cards.

title: FRAD067-114 Papiers militaires d'Anton Steyer, né à Lemberg (Moselle) en 1875 (armée allemande) | Miltary documents, postcards, photographs and prayer book from Anton Steyer
text: Photograph and driving certificate dating back to Anton Steyer's first period of service (1897-1899); military booklets (Militärpass and Soldbuch); photo card received from his brother (27/05/1916, Bischwiller (Bas-Rhin), 1. Rekr. Depot 2. Ersatz Battalion Infanterie R. 138); photo card sent to his wife (married in 1907) and his 3 children (27/02/1917, Russia); family photography (nd); fighter card; Feldgesangbuch (64 pages)

title: Mon grand-père paternel poilu de 14-18 | Charles Georges Richard Pauquet's Pictures from the War
text: - Photo top left: my grandfather (cross) and other soldiers are resting in front of the entrance to a trench, smoking a pipe or a cigarette. Photo taken in 1914 or early 1915 because they wear kepis and not helmets. you will notice the puttees and the big shoes. - Photo (cropped) in the center: another moment of relaxation, freshening up in front of the drinking trough, my grandfather in the background, same dates. - Photo bottom left: the same on the beach in Cherbourg - Photo top right: he is obviously posing for the photographer while scanning the sky with binoculars (enemy plane in sight?) - Photo bottom right: confers with his comrades in the shelter of a hedge of fir trees

title: FRAN-PI-38 Histoire du volontaire Marcel Jean Eugène Migot | Marcel Migot' story
text: - Photographs - "Logbook" = story between 1915 and 1918 (one of which was published in a newspaper) - Certificate of good conduct - Volunteer combatant card - Identification and services report - Copy of a clinical "report"

title: FRAD062_019 - Jules Level, 1914. | FRAD062_019 - Jules Level, 1914.
text: - Two photographs by Jules Level. - Postcard of Jules Level from Camp Chalon.

title: FRAM - Xavier Cambon, un observateur photographe | Documents from Xavier Cambon
text: 1. Photograph of Xavier Cambon in aviator's uniform (nd). 2. Photograph of Xavier Cambon aboard an airplane (nd). 3. Logbook (1916-1918). 4. Mention in Order of the Division (1917).

title: Sammlung von Feldpostkarten, Fotos und Dokumenten verschiedener Personen | Collection of Fieldpost, photos and documents of different persons
text: Numerous photos, documents and field postcards, including a certificate of award for wearing the Iron Cross 2nd Class of Corporal Borchert dated May 15, 1918.

title: FRAD087_042-Avant le départ pour la guerre. Juillet 1914. Jean MONTASTIER | Jean Monstatier's photography
text: Photograph of the saddlers-saddlers before their departure for war in July 1914. Jean Montastier is seated in the first row at the bottom, in 3rd position from the left.

title: FRAM - Quatre frères au combat | Four brothers in Great War
text: Photographs, military booklets, Yves Le Grand's correspondence, army correspondence, mobilization booklet, information sheet, Julien Le Grand's notebook and photographs, identification medallion.

title: FRAM - Jean-Baptiste Potier (1896-1966) | Document from Jean-Baptiste Potier
text: Photograph of Jean-Baptiste Potier and certificate of war injuries. Photograph of a transport crate in the name of Jean-Baptiste Potier. Photograph of an axe.

title: L' affaire du Bonnet d'évêque, le 11 décembre 1914 | The Bishop's Bonnet Affair, December 11, 1914 the Life of Edmond Schaeffer
text: - Photos of a wooden frame forming a display, containing the military decorations of our grandfather Edmond SCHAEFFER and a medallion representing him on one side, and on the other, the portrait of his daughter Georgette BISCHOFF, née SCHAEFFER - The photos of the 11 pages of the manuscript relating the fight of SAINT-JULIEN. - A photo of a map of the northeast of the YPRES region mentioning the WINCHESTER FARM. - Research sheet, made by a friend: MC LAMAISON. - Wedding photo of Edmond and Julienne SCHAEFFER - September 12, 1910. - Death certificate of Edmond SCHAEFFER.

title: FRAD083_042 - Jean-Marie Georges Marvoyer | Jean-Marie Georges Marvoyer, French soldier
text: Photograph Military booklet torn by shrapnel Military booklet (duplicate)

title: FRAM - François Henri Morisset (1870-1958), un peintre au front | Documents from François Henri Morisset
text: 1. Watercolor representing a soldier in the middle of ruins (1916). 2. Watercolor depicting wood fetching in winter in the Apremont forest (1917). 3. Sketch of the painting “Le Pèpère” (nd).

title: FRAD066_098b Léon Bultel | Documents from Léon Bultel
text: Collection of poems by Léon Bultel (selection); photograph of Léon Bultel with his wife and son.

title: FRBMBX-058 - Fernand Caron, le courage d'un soldat ordinaire | Fernand Caron, a brave simple soldier
text: - Campaign notebook: first entry - Campaign notebook: testament - Letter from R. Watel to the family of Fernand Caron (17/04/1919). Account of hospitalization and death.

title: FRAD008_001 Une famille d'Hannogne-Saint-Martin dans la guerre | Documents from Genesseau family
text: Diary kept by Henri Genesseau. Notebook found in the bag of Charles Genesseau at his death. Correspondence exchanged between Charles and his wife Marthe.

title: Gefreiter Fritz Düngel, Bursche von Major Wilhelm von Ditfurth | Fritz Düngel under the command of Major Wilhelm von Ditfurth
text: Album with field postcards and photos by Fritz Düngel. Depicted in the photos are, inter alia, Major Wilhelm v. Ditfurth, Grand Duke of Baden at the troop parade in the Billy Gorge on July 7th, 1915, Major vd Busche, several unnamed generals and the locations: Metz, Billy, Combres, Havrincourt Castle, Longeville, St. Quentin, Le Cateau.

title: FRAD083_41_Joseph Emile Bournadet | Joseph Emile Bournadet, french soldier
text: Letters from Joseph Emile Bournadet written in the trenches to his wife Marguerite, the last of which remains unfinished. A photograph of Joseph Bournadet.

title: FRAD078-01_1 Léon Ottenheim, la passion de la photographie | Léon Ottenheim, fond of photography
text: Glass plates and their boxes, presenting the ruins of the city of Reims after the war, as well as family photos.

title: FRAD017 - Histoire de Charles Jaffard | Charles Jaffard
text: Citations, medals, postcard from his regiment

title: FRAD087_086-Correspondance du soldat Mousnier | Story of the soldier Jean-Martin Mousnier
text: Three postcards addressed by Jean Martin Mousnier to his sister Jeanne (1915)

title: FRAD062_075 - Charles Louis (1896-1955). | Document from Louis Charles (1896-1955)
text: - Letter. - Shooting book. - Passes. -List of men composing the third piece of artillery.

title: FRAD066_101B Pierre CODERC | Documents of Pierre CODERC
text: A photograph of Marguerite AUBERT's maternal grandparents taken one day on leave from Pierre CODERC; a postcard dated September 11, 1916 addressed to his sister-in-law in which he gave his news and thanked her for his package.

title: AMSM-019_2 : Marius et Léon JAYNE | Marius and Léon Jayne at the Front
text: - 1 photo of the two JAYNE brothers in Bonifacio during their military class - 4 letters

title: FRAM - Amédée Desforges, soldat téléphoniste au 165e régiment d'infanterie | Documents about Amédée Desforges
text: 1. Photograph of Amédée Desforges (seated) with two regimental comrades (nd). 2. Mention in the order of the regiment (1918) 3. Diploma commemorating the battles of Verdun (1917) 4. Veteran's kepi plate (?).

title: Ledermappe mit Fotos und Dokumenten von Wilhelm Große Munkenbeck | Leathercase with photos and documents of Wilhelm Große Munkenbeck
text: (1) small leather folder for storing photos, postcards and other documents. (2) Photos, including with a company, under the Christmas tree, from the "Abguldener See" and various other motifs as well as a field postcard (3) New Year's card and invitation card to an "autumn celebration at 286" on October 26, 1918 (4) Points card from an English card game named "Trump Whist" (5) obituary by unknown

title: FRAD062_107 - Carnel Ulysse, 1911-1916. | FRAD062_107 - Ulysse Carnel, 1911-1919.
text: - 1 citation to the order of the army corps, July 18, 1916. - 1 citation to the order of the brigade, December 1, 1916. - Death notice dated March 15, 1917. - Military booklet. - Artillery notebook. - Letter addressed to his father Jules Carnel concerning the allowances sent following death in action, dated April 15, 1917. - Certificate addressed to his mother Sidonie Carnel concerning the allowances sent following death in action, dated January 9, 1919, following the death of her husband. - Pocket watch returned to the family with Ulysse's personal effects following his death. - 7 photographs.

title: FRAD066_102 Charles MIRC | Documents of Charles MIRC
text: Charles MIRC, maternal grandfather of Marie SOLAZ, is from Pia. A winegrower before the war, he became a policeman after the war for fifteen years, then resumed his activity as a winegrower. He fought in the Vosges in the 253rd infantry regiment.

title: FRAD071-007, Charles QUELIN | Documents from Charles Quelin
text: A photo, a letter and an extract from the registration number.

title: FRAD043_065 Louis Jonget, Paul et Simon Borel, soldats de la Grande guerre. | Documents about Louis Jonget, Paul et Simon Borel
text: 1/ Photograph of Louis Jonget in the 1st row seated, second from the left (1914-1916). 2/ Postcard from Louis Jonget to his mother. Louis announces his departure for the front, “the time to go to the front has arrived” (August 3, 1916). 3/ Two postcards from Louis Jonget to his mother. Louis explains to his mother that he is very happy to leave Bourges quickly because normally his unit should have received "an instruction concerning the crapouillots de tranches" (mortars) and go to the front line in stride. Luckily, three batteries asked for reinforcements to serve the 75 guns (Bourges August 4, 1914). 4/ Postcard from Louis Jonget to his mother. Louis describes life in the trenches: "when the guns fire, we have our work, when there is rest we make shelters, we put earth, corrugated iron, all night we receive shells (Somme, August 20, 1916). 5/ Photograph of Louis Jonget in a Parisian hospital, his arm injured, in the company of the Princess Nurse Lucinge-Faucigny. Louis is the 4th from the left. Publication of the magazine Sur le vive (January 20, 1917). 6/ Photograph of Paul and Simon Borel taken by M.-M. Vessiere (Baccarat, 1914).

title: FRAM - Histoire de Georges Chasserot (1891-1978) | Documents from Georges Chasserot
text: 1. Photograph of Georges Chasserot at Solesmes in the Sarthe, seated, first from the left (1915). 2. Photograph of Georges Chasserot in military uniform (nd). 3. Mention in Order of the Division (1915). 4. Citation to the Order of the Army Corps (1917). 5. Hospital ticket (1917). 6. X-ray sketch (nd).

title: Fliegerhelm von Wilhelm Große Munkenbeck | Pilots helmet of Wilhelm Große Munkenbeck
text: Leather aviator helmet by Wilhelm Große Munkenbeck.

title: FRAM - Ernest LORRAIN | Ernest LORRAIN
text: - Photograph of Ernest LORRAIN as a cuirassier - Photograph of Ernest LORRAIN at the front - Mention in the order of the division of Ernest LORRAIN (February 10, 1921) - Diploma giving the right to wear Fourragère by Ernest LORRAIN (January 1919 )

title: FRAD034-037_Joseph VIDAL et Aimé HYACINTHE | Documents from Joseph Vidal and Aimé Hyacinthe
text: Joseph VIDAL left the following documents and objects: - a wooden and metal sewing kit; - a case embroidered by a nurse, from the military hospital where he was treated. It bears an embroidered and painted inscription ("Hospital 101 / Christmas 1914 / Prop patria!"), as well as a French flag and a plant motif. Joseph VIDAL maintained a correspondence with the nurse after the war; - two photographs in uniform: one during his military service with the 143rd in Carcassonne according to the caption on the back; the other, when he is older standing next to a chair; - his military booklet; - an official letter giving him the right to wear the Victory Medal, dated November 17, 1920. Aimé HYACINTHE left two postcards: one dated September 5, 1914; the other, April 28, 1917, part of Creil, addressed to his brothers and sisters.

title: AMSM-016_1 Lettres à Amélie | Letters From Louis Anselmet to his Sister
text: 3 Louis Anselmet photographs 9 correspondence on postcards

title: AMSM 016-2 Recueil de lettres | Letters of André Larfeuil, POW in Germany
text: _ choice of about twenty letters _ a photo of André Larfeuil prisoner

title: FRAD085-014 Journal de guerre d'Alfred Bonnaud | Alfred Bonnaud's diary
text: Alfred Bonnaud wrote a war diary "in 1921 [based on] very brief notes, taken day by day, sometimes during the fight, and above all [from] memories". He precisely describes the movements, the military operations, the daily activities, the destruction, leaving little room for his personal impressions.

title: FRAM - Henri Bionnet | Documents from Henri Bionnet
text: Small manuscript notebook of about 75 pages

title: FRAD058_042 M. Abel BLOND, M. Marius ARBLAY. | Documents from Mr Labbe
text: Mr. Pierre LABBE lent documents concerning the soldiers: Abel Blond and Marius Arblay.

title: FRAM - Louis PRUNET (1881-1951) | Louis PRUNET
text: Original certificate of war injury.

title: FRBMAN-012 Antoine Laurenty | Antoine Laurenty's story
text: - 2 linen-backed notebooks constituting Antoine Laurenty's diary - 1 digitization of the cover, prefaces, foreword and post-face of the 2003 edition of Antoine Laurenty's notebooks - 1 photograph of Antoine Laurenty, his wife Mathilde and her sister-in-law - 1 photograph of René Sibenaler in the trenches of the Yser - 10 small photographs concerning René Sibenaler at the front

title: FRBMAN-013-Les deux frères François et Jean-Baptiste Moreau | Two brothers, François and Jean-Baptiste Moreau
text: -1 notebook containing the war diary of the Moreau brothers -1 postcard sent on September 6, 1914 from Semoy (45) by François Moreau to his parents

title: FRBMAN-014 Justin Usse, élève pilote au centre d'aviation militaire de Chartres | Justin Usse, fighter pilot
text: -Military booklet by Justin Usse -Pilot booklet by Justin Usse -Album of photographs compiled after the war by Justin Usse (photographs of planes, aerial topographical surveys, portraits) -Letter from the Ministry of War (03/04/1920) for award of the Distinguished Conduct Medal (English Decoration)

title: Souvenirs et notes du cuirassier Jacques PAVARD | Memories and notes from cuirassier Jacques PAVARD
text: Transcription of the war diaries with insertion of some photos, postcards and documents found with the diaries.

title: FRAD067-198 Un jeune engagé volontaire lyonnais dans la guerre, Anthelme Perrin (1895-1979) | Documents from Anthelme Perrin
text: The documentation kept by Mr. Daniel Perocheau, who knew Anthelme Perrin well before his death in Marseilles on August 10, 1979, consists of a photograph of the soldier with his family (nd); a safe-conduct from 1916 and two travel authorizations from 1916 and 1917; an original letter; an information sheet from 1917; two notebooks of letters to his parents and his sister Julie Jeanne Perrin (Lyon, 12/12/1898-06/24/1945), copied by his mother and respectively covering the periods from 07/29/1914 to 09/19/ 1916 and from 03/01/1916 to 05/19/1916; a meal card from 1919; a military booklet. Only these last two pieces have not been reproduced for their anecdotal nature or in anticipation of the online distribution of the military registration registers of the classes that took part in the Great War. The notebooks were digitized by extracts: 26 first letters of the 1st notebook, documenting the first 4 months of war, and fragment of diary of the summer of 1915 copied at the top of the 2nd notebook.

title: Juste à temps! | A mother journeys to see her injured son, and attends his funeral
text: - Letter from Susie Crombie to her husband Bob: this letter is typewritten, on airmail paper, and written in Folkestone on July 14, 1917; Susie recounts her arrival, her journey to London, how she learns of the death of her son at the IOD Hospital, then his military funeral the next day, in a cemetery, no doubt in London (located 3/4 of an hour from Folkestone), buried with three other soldiers, two Canadians and an Australian, in the presence of several people including Colonel Clarke, of the 45th Battalion to which Lieutenant Charlie CS Crombie belonged. -Official letter announcing to Susie Crombie the death of her son Vincent. - Photo of Charlie's gravestone. - Photo of Vincent's tombstone.

title: FRBNBU-051 Pascal Thuillier très gravement blessé en septembre 1914 | Pascal Thuillier seriously injured in 1914
text: - Account notebook of his war with photos during his care in Vichy and poem for the nurses with poem of response from the nurses - Photo of the four brothers who left for the war.

title: AMSM-027_1 : Documents de mémoire | Nazaire François Prat's Medical Records from WWI
text: 8 pieces concerning injury, evacuation, reform centers, convalescent leave.

title: FRAD017 - Histoire de Louis Rouillon | Histoire de Louis Rouillon
text: 1 road journal (1914), 1 photo of the regiment, class 1912-1913, 1 photograph of Emile Rouillon (1914)

title: FRAD043 - Louis Gérentes, cycliste du colonel | Louis Gérentes, colonel's cyclist
text: Correspondence of Louis Gérentes with his wife Joséphine and his daughters

title: FRAD078-05 Le décès du capitaine Edmond Rousseau | Death of Edmond Rousseau
text: Barrack plan, administrative documents, photographs.

title: Dokumente meines Großvaters. Einblicke in das Leben des Soldaten Kurt Willy Illing an der Ost- und Westfront | Documents from my grandfather. About the life of the soldier Kurt Willy Illing
text: Kurt Willy Illing was born in Neuschoenburg in 1893 and fought on both the western and eastern fronts during World War I. After the war he successfully applied for the "German Field Honor Sign".

title: FRAD062_097 - Henri Sinthomez. | FRAD062_097 - Henri Sinthomez.
text: - Photograph by Henry Sinthomez. - Photograph of the family of Henry Sinthomez. - Two memorial diplomas of "Mort pour la France" in the name of Henri Sinthomez.

title: FRAD067-102 Albert Daltroff, Mosellan engagé dans l'armée allemande, correspond avec sa fiancée | Documents from Jean Daltroff
text: 6 postcards sent between 1915 and 1918 to Léa Jacob in Vantoux (Wanton bei Metz).

title: FRAD066-105-Félix ROCACHER | Documents of Félix Rocacher
text: Letters. A letter explains the details of the circumstances of Felix's death in the trenches in a detailed, military style. Another written by a comrade describes the same facts but in another style (ROUX) A posthumous letter from the sergeant (L.MASSON) addressed to the family of Félix. 2 photographs.

title: FRAD067-167 Franz Xavier Host, Alsacien engagé dans l'armée allemande, correspond avec les siens | Documents from Gérard Host
text: correspondence (postcards, photo cards) photographs personal notes soldier's cross and diploma leaflet press clipping

title: FRAM - Pierre Coeffic | Document about Pierre Coeffic
text: Photograph of Pierre Coeffic and his 5 medals.

title: AMSM-025_1 : Sept ans sous les drapeaux | Célestin Joseph Baptistin's Story from the Great War
text: - 4 photo-cards of Célestin Boyer, soldier. - copies and extracts from the Registrar Matriculations: class 1913 /Nice/N° 831, pages 194-95-96 (in Var Departmental Archives) - copy of a summons to the order of the regiment. - copy of the song book, addressed to Marie Perrone..

title: Besitzzeugnis über das Verdienstkreuz für Kriegshilfe von Franz Schönmehl | Certificate for decoration of Franz Schönmehl
text: (1) Certificate of possession of the Cross of Merit for War Aid from Vizefeldwebel d. L. Franz Schönmehl. The certificate was issued in Berlin on December 21, 1917 and, according to the information on the back, handed over on August 3, 1918 and provided with a stamp from the "Chief of the field railway system". (2) Photo by Franz Schönmehl

title: FRAM - Laurent Tromeur, mémoires d'un poilu | Documents from Jean Tromeur
text: Photograph of Laurent Tromeur and his family Memoirs of Laurent Tromeur

title: FRAM - Journal de guerre de Jean Carré | Document from Jean Corentin Carré
text: Journal of Jean Carré Press cutting with the letter of Jean Carré, addressed to Colonel Treillard, published in the newspaper Le Petit Parisein on July 8, 1917

title: FRAD078- 08 Félix TERRON et François GURDIN dans la guerre 1914-1918 | Félix TERRON et François GURDIN in the war
text: - A photographic card of the German family where my grandfather was a prisoner, - a pen holder made from shells - letter-envelopes from François GURDIN to his sisters, with a paragraph written in sympathetic ink (censorship) - 3 postcards, correspondence of François GURDIN with his sisters

title: FRAD004-002 François Carraz blessé pendant la guerre | Medical cards
text: War diary written in ink pencil, small gifts from wartime godmothers, photographs taken at the hospital, correspondence.

title: Een brief naar het thuisfront van Petrus Franciscus Marivoet | Letter of war veteran Pieter Frans Marivoet to his family
text: A letter from Petrus Franciscus Marivoet to the home front.

title: Schilderij van de kerk van Grimde | Grimde
text: Painting of the church of Grimde.

title: FRAD017 - Histoire de Paul Emile Larcher | Histoire de Paul Emile Larcher
text: 1 military booklet, 1 photograph, 1 certificate.

title: FRAM - Carte postale de Charles Sarret | Charles Sarret
text: postcard representing the destroyed Reims cathedral

title: FRAM - La mort au feu du sous-officier Fernand GAILLON à Beaumont-sur-Argonne (20 août 1917) | Fernand GAILLON
text: - Extracts from the album of photographs taken and captioned by Fernand GAILLON himself (1915-1916)

title: FRBNBU-090 Amédé Fleury photographe | Amédé Fleury's story
text: Annette Bon, contributor, comes from Marie-Madeleine Fleury (born in 1923), only daughter of Amédé Fleury (December 1887 in Lomme (Nord)-July 1966). Amédé Fleury was a teacher at the Ecole Michelet. He spent his leave at the Carmel of Compiègne in the company of his aunt. He was a sergeant in the 43rd line. As a teacher, he became a vaguemestre. He also writes for other soldiers. He has a camera with which he photographed "his" war; he develops his photographs and mentions on the back of each the dates, places, characters and events to which they refer like a logbook from 1914 to 1919. He is in the occupied zone (Lille and Lomme). Some of his photographs serve him as a support for correspondence with his wife, Marie-Madeleine Fleury.

title: FRBNBU-112 Abraham Florent Villain dit André Villain | Abraham Florent Villain alias André Villain
text: 01-Notebook "The Victory Notebook" (1919) gathers information on Abraham Florent Villain dit André Villain; the notebook bears the usual information (civil status, assignment, civil correspondents, citations, permissions, body changes); however, the notebook is peppered with eccentric indications added in black ink, surely later (!). 02-Leaflet folded in the format of the notebook (inserted in this one) "Chant des Volontaires" dedicated by the 1st Company to Our Commander.

title: AMSM-020_1 : Jean Marcadal | Jean Marcadal
text: _a photo _1 description of the services requested.

title: FRAD041 - Thomas Fouineteau, soldat de la Grande guerre | Letters from Thomas Fouineteau
text: Copy of the active correspondence of Thomas Fouineteau

title: AMSM-020_3 : Antoine-Marc Giorgetti | Antoine-Marc Giorgetti on board the ''Diane''
text: _ a reward telegram _ report on the attack 2 pages _ letter from the Ministry of the Navy for citation

title: FRAD017 - Histoire d'André Brillet | Histoire d'André Brillet
text: 1 military booklet, 1 certificate of registration in the Verdun book, 1 citation to the order of the 169th infantry regiment, 1 diploma of the Voluntary Combatant's Cross, 1 certificate of good conduct, 1 commemorative medal diploma for operations carried out in Syria and the Levant, 1 service report, 1 demobilization certificate, 2 repatriation certificates.

title: AMSM-015-2 FANTINO Emile Le maréchal ferrant | Émile Louis Constant Fantino
text: - 1 military booklet belonging to FONTINO Emile - 1 Certificate of good behavior - 1 leave: a permission - 1 travel voucher - photos: postcards addressed to his parents

title: FRBMLI-047 Carnets de mon arrière-grand-père, Léon Fernand Lemeré | Story of Léon Fernand Lemeré
text: 4 war diaries kept from 1914 to 1917 (missing the year 1918 announced, but lost) by Léon Fernand Lemeré containing his diary, the list of correspondence sent and received, expenses, songs and notes. An aluminum ring with the embedded portrait of her daughter, Marcelle (my grandmother) born in September 1913. Photographs by Fernand Lemeré.

title: FRBNBU-153 Marcel Amédée Pellet a connu sa femme grâce à une blessure de guerre | Marcel Amédée Mamet's wife known during the war
text: 01- Frame of Marcel Pellet in photo with his crutches and his war cross. This frame remained prominently in his apartment until the end of his life. 02- Map in watercolor and ink, drawn by L. Blain "1st flag taken from the enemy" August 24, 1914. 03- Map in watercolor and ink, drawn by L. Blain, "3rd flag taken from the enemy" 1914 04- Map in watercolor and ink, drawn by L. Blain, "shooting on a German plane" 1914 05- Three postcards of the village of Gland destroyed after the second battle of the Marne in 1918.

title: FRAD006_01NUM_0093 Histoire de Louis Guavarry | Fonds Mme Denise Boudry
text: Louis Guavarry, born October 18, 1895 in Saint-Vallier-de-Thiey (department of Alpes-Maritimes), will be a gunner in the navy aboard the “Charlemagne” then on the auxiliary cruiser “Provence II” (1914-1916). He disappeared with his ship on February 26, 1916, off the coast of the Peloponnese. He is the great-grandfather of the applicant.

title: FRAD006_01NUM_0094 Histoire d’Honoré Bousquias | Fonds Jackie et Jaqueline Marino
text: Honoré Bousquias was born in April 1875 in Grasse. Died on 08/17/1956 in Antibes. Trader by profession. His bracelet-registration bears his class and his registration "Bousquias Honoré 1895 / Nice 1171". He is the paternal grandfather of the applicant, Jackie Marino (son of Honoré Marino, himself son of Honoré Bousquias and Lucie Marino)

title: AMSM-023_1 : ROUBERT Louis mon grand-père | Shell Artwork Belonging to Louis Roubert
text: - 4 sculpted shells from 1915

title: Feldpostbriefe meines Vaters Hanns Schäfer aus dem 1. Weltkrieg | field post of my father Hanns Schäfer
text: - Selection of field post letters from Hanns Schäfer to his parents from 1914 - 1 original field post letter dated August 25, 1914 - 1 photo of Hanns Schäfer August 26, 1914 Ludwigsburg - Report on the Somme battle of June 24 - July 24, 1916

title: Omer Martin, soldat Maître-ouvrier du Génie, 1914-1919 | Omer Martin, Soldier Master Engineer, 1914-1919
text: Postcards (correspondence with his family), photographs, War Cross, diploma of the order of citation (1917), veteran's card (1935)

title: FRAD008_3 Documents trouvés par M. Bonalda | Documents found by M. BONALDA
text: Notes from Abbé BOUCHER, from Issancourt. Entry of the Germans into Mohon, by Pierre FAUVILLE. Story by Adolphe OUICE, reservist at the 247th line. Name of the Germans staying in Charleville, with their address. Response letter from the King of Spain to the request for the release of Marcel DROUART.

title: FRAD078- 09 Mon grand- père et parain, Claude Mercier, blessé de guerre. | My grandfather and godfather, Claude Mercier
text: Administrative documents and black and white photographs.

title: FRBMBX-056/ brothers in war | Brothers Petit in war
text: Doc 1 photo of Numa Petit, photo of cigarette case and compass Doc 2-4 photo of Guillaume, military medals, diploma

title: FRAD062_047 - Léonide Thieullez, 1914. | Documents from Léonide Thieullez, 1914
text: - Photograph of Henri Thieullez taken before the start of the war. - Letter from the International Committee of the Red Cross to Rosa Legrand-Thieullez.

title: FRAM - Histoire de Jean Adolphe et Zoé GUIGUET-DORON | Documents from Jean-Adolphe et Zoé GUIGUET-DORON
text: 1 - Photograph and medals of Jean Adolphe Guiguet-Doron (war cross). 2 - Letter card from Private Lacroix to Zoé Guiguet-Doron, November 27, 1915.

title: FRAD008_54 Alliance franco-américaine | Photograph from Baleux family
text: Couple of civilians welcoming two American soldiers in Brieulles-sur-Bar

title: FRBMLI-024 André VAN EYCKE, Maréchal des Logis, tué en 1916 à Maurepas | Story of André VAN EYCKE
text: Last letter from André Van Eycke dated 20 09 1916. Official letter dated 16-10-1916 announcing his death. Photograph of André Van Eycke in military uniform (1913). Citation of André Van Eycke to the Order of the Army Corps.

title: FRAD080 - Les mémoires de guerre d' Emile Degand | Emile Degand's diary
text: Excerpt from the first notebook of Emile Degand's memoirs.

title: FRAM - Histoire de Paul et Eugène ANGELIER | Documents from Paul ANGELIER
text: 1 – Letters from Paul Angelier to his brother Eugène, Moudros May 17 - October 27, 1915. 2 - Letter from Paul Angelier to his brother Eugène, Marseilles, April 29, 1916. 3 - Letter from Paul Angelier to his brother Eugène, Marseilles, May 26, 1916. 4 – Letter from Paul Angelier to his brother Eugène, Baugy, February 10, 1917. 5 - Card from Paul Angelier to his parents, March 19, 1917.

title: FRBNBU-080 Joseph Antoine Callet, photographe de l'escadrille des cigognes | Joseph Antoine Callet, photograh of "escadrille des cigognes"
text: - 14 photographs of the Cigognes squadron and its members, including Joseph Antoine Callet, Georges Guynemer, Charles Nungesser, and the squadron's mascot dog.

title: FRAD058_034 M.Joseph TARDIVON, M. Jean-Marie TARDIVON, Léon TARDIVON | Documents from Mr Tardivon
text: Mr. Bernard Tardivon lent various documents concerning the following soldiers: Joseph Tardivon Jean-Marie TARDIVON Léon Tardivon.

title: FRAD067-124 Jacques et Emile Humann, d'Entzheim (Bas-Rhin) : deux frères sous les armes (armée allemande) | Postcards from Jacques and Emile Humann
text: Military papers of Jacques Humann (Soldbuch, Entlassungsschein, Militärdienstzeitbescheinigung, 3 veteran cards), 1 photograph, 50 postcards addressed by Jacques Humann to his father Daniel Humann (1914-1918), 8 postcards received by Jacques Humann (1914 -1918), 13 undated unwritten photo cards (mainly Macedonia), 5 cards sent by Emilie Humann to her father Daniel Humann, 8 cards received by Emile Humann (including 3 cards from his brother Jacques). Among the documents on loan, only a notebook was not digitized (Kriegserlebnisse, 05/08/1914-25/03/1916).

title: FRAM - Eugène BERTRAND (1876-1918) | Eugène BERTRAND
text: War album of photographs taken between 1916 and 1917 on the battalion fields, before and after the attacks but also on leave (1916-1917). State of services (1920).

title: FRAD087_041. Soldat Jean BOUDOU. | Documents from Jean Boudou
text: An extract from a medical book (1918), an anti-typhoid vaccination card (1917), a photo taken with his wife before he left for the war (1914).

title: FRAD008_12 Sergent Léon Jacquemart, 418e régiment d'infanterie | Sergent Léon JACQUEMART, 418th infantry regiment
text: Citation to the order of the regiment, photograph, drawing (portrait).

title: FRAM - Histoire de Firmin, Marie-Louise, Emile et Cécile ALLAMAND | Documents from ALLAMAND family
text: 1 – Letter and card from Firmin Allamand to Marie-Louise Allamand, Albertville, August 2, 1914. 2 – Letter from Emile Allamand to Firmin Allamand, Bellentre, July 12, 1915. 3 – Family portrait taken June 14, 1916 at the favor of permission: Firmin, Marie-Louise, Emile and Cécile Allamand. 4 – Double letter, from Marie-Louise Allamand to her husband, Bellentre on August 19, 1916 and in return from Firmin Allamand to his wife, on August 23, 1916. He slips a piece of newspaper to indicate his position. He is killed the next day. 5 – Last letters from Marie-Louise Allamand to Firmin Allamand.

title: FRAD008_16 Vie et mort du cannonier Emmanuel Guégain | Life and death of gunner Emmanuel GUEGAIN
text: Drawing number to the review board, letter to his parents, letter announcing his death.

title: FRBMBX-010 Histoire de Fernand Greteau | Fernand Greteau's story
text: 1/ Postcard "In the Argonne" double-sided 2/ Mentions to the order of the regiment 3/ Postcard of the 32nd Dragoons double-sided

title: FRAM - Emile Maurice PORTET (1879-1947) | Emile PORTET
text: Extract from Emile Portet's military booklet. Extracts from the war diary kept from 1914 to 1918. Photo-postcard of the officers of the 5th Company of the 87th RI, in November 1914. Photographs on the battlefields [1916]. Portait d'Emile Portet, in his officer's uniform, post-war. Mention in the Order of the Legion of Honor (1916).

title: FRAM - Histoire d'Émile Fristo | Documents from Émile Fristo
text: 1. Postcard written to Émile Fristo by Antoine Gilain his brother-in-law (March 1915). 2. Postcard written by Émile Fristo (3rd from left) to his wife (nd). 3. Postcard representing Émile Fristo, then in care at the Châteauroux hospital (nd).

title: FRAM - Joseph Horel (1897-1973), cavalier au 2e régiment de chasseurs à cheval | Documents from Joseph Horel
text: Number sheet Extract from the census register of the class of 1917 Table of medals

title: FRBMAN-004 Soldat Pierre Auguste Gabillard | Soldier Pierre Auguste Gabillard
text: Pierre Auguste Gabillard was born on October 15, 1889 in La Chappelle-Glain in Loire Atlantique and died for France on October 13, 1918 in Sainte-Marie-à-Py in Marne. He belongs to the 22nd Infantry Regiment.
